name: Curried Sweet Potato Squares with Peanut Phyllo Crust
recipeInstructions:
Make the filling:
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Prick potatoes and bake in the middle of the oven for 1 1/2 hours, or until very soft. Scoop the flesh into a bowl and beat in the remaining filling ingredients with salt to taste. The filling may be made up to 2 days ahead and chilled, covered.
Reduce oven temperature to 425°F.
In a small bowl, stir together butter and curry powder. Place one phyllo sheet on a work surface with the long side facing you. Brush the left half of the phyllo with some curry butter and sprinkle with about 1 teaspoon peanuts. Fold the plain half over and fold in each end to make an 8-inch square. Brush the top with curry butter and sprinkle with about 1 teaspoon peanuts.
Arrange the phyllo in an 8-inch square baking pan. Fold the butter, and sprinkle two more phyllo sheets in the same manner, arranging them in the baking pan as prepared. Spread the filling on the bottom crust. Fold the butter, and sprinkle the remaining three phyllo sheets in the same manner, arranging over the filling to form the top crust and invert the last sheet so that the folded-over phyllo is on the underside.
Bake the sweet potato mixture in the middle of the oven for 20 minutes, or until the crust is flaky and golden. Cut into squares.
